title: $1,000 in NASDAQ-100 After Dot-Com Crash (2003)
description: What $1,000 invested in the NASDAQ-100 (via QQQ ETF) on January 2, 2003 — after the index had fallen ~80% from its dot-com peak — would be worth today. This was the deepest and most feared market in a generation. Few investors had the courage to buy.
dividend_note: QQQ pays modest quarterly dividends. DRIP assumed.
context_note: The NASDAQ-100 peaked at ~4,816 in March 2000 and bottomed at ~804 in October 2002. This scenario starts near the bottom, about 3 months before the final trough.
educational_note: Buying after an 80% crash felt terrifying. The companies that survived the dot-com bust — Apple, Amazon, Google, Microsoft — went on to dominate the global economy. QQQ is essentially a basket of those survivors and successors.
